title: slirp4netns cpu usage with torrents
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Running an image for deluge with some torrent downloading (~20MB/s), slirp4netns takes around 30-50% cpu according to `top`. Is it an expecting behavior with all the tcp connections involved by torrents ? Could it be related to https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns/issues/157 ?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pod create --name mypod -p 8112:8112`

2. `podman create --name=deluge --pod mypod linuxserver/deluge`

3. `podman start deluge`

4. go to `http://localhost:8112`, password is `deluge`. Set download path to `/downloads` and load a torrent, like https://torrent.fedoraproject.org/torrents/Fedora-Workstation-Live-x86_64-31.torrent for instance.

**Describe the results you received:**

Cpu usage of slirp4netns is around 35% while deluged consumes 65% for instance. (34 connections to peers and downloading at ~25MB/s)

**Describe the results you expected:**

Only deluged usage would be noticeable in the `top` output.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.6.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.13.1
  podman version: 1.6.2
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.11.3
  CgroupVersion: v2
  Conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.2-1.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.2, commit: 186a550ba0866ce799d74006dab97969a2107979'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "31"
  IDMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  MemFree: 141193216
  MemTotal: 8212029440
  OCIRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.10.6-1.fc31.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.10.6
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  SwapFree: 8202743808
  SwapTotal: 8346660864
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: nuc
  kernel: 5.3.16-300.fc31.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  slirp4netns:
    Executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    Package: slirp4netns-0.4.0-20.1.dev.gitbbd6f25.fc31.x86_64
    Version: |-
      slirp4netns version 0.4.0-beta.3+dev
      commit: bbd6f25c70d5db2a1cd3bfb0416a8db99a75ed7e
  uptime: 28m 56.36s
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
  - quay.io
store:
  ConfigFile: [HOME]/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 6
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: fuse-overlayfs-0.7.2-2.fc31.x86_64
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.6.2
        fuse-overlayfs: version 0.7.2
        FUSE library version 3.6.2
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.29
  GraphRoot: [HOME]/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 7
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
  VolumePath: [HOME]/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.6.2-2.fc31.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fedora Server 31 on a baremetal

comments.body:
Slirp is expected to use some CPU (it has to manually move data into and
out of the container) but that amount sounds high. How fast are you
downloading at?

On Sat, Dec 21, 2019, 17:10 Geoffrey Bonneville <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub

I was downloading at around `25MB/s`, not so fast I guess. Is it expected ?

Changing mtu may have some effect?

https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/b29928f01598810328fa6b0cf91abb84670d8d01/libpod/networking_linux.go#L212
I Just compiled the project with mtu set to 1500, result is similar. Any other guess ? The issue is easily reproductible, I updated the original post with more details if you want to give it a try.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think that CPU usage is expected when using slirp4netns as it is emulating the entire network stack.
Closing the issue as it's expected behavior.
